devanagari: अक्रूरकृता भगवत् स्तुति । श्रीअक्रूर उवाच । नतोऽस्म्यहं त्वाखिलहेतुहेतुं । नारायणं पूरुषमाद्यमव्ययम् । यन्नाभिजातादरविन्दकोषाद् । ब्रह्...
english_devnagari: akrūrakṛtā bhagavat stutiśrīakrūra uvācanato'smyahaṃ tvākhilahetuhetuṃnārāyaṇaṃ pūruṣamādyamavyayamyannābhijātādaravindakoṣādbrahmā'virāsīd yata eṣa lokaḥ
translation:
Akrūra said:
I bow down to you as you are the Prime cause of all causes (like Mahat etc. from which the Universe evolved). You are the veritable Nārāyaṇa (the Deity reposing over the cosmic waters), the In-ḍweller in all bodies, the First Principle not subject to change or modification. It is from the lotus sprouted from his navel that god Brahmā, the Creator of the Universe appeared. It is from him (Brahmā) that the universe was evolved.

devanagari: भूस्तोयमग्निः पवनं खमादिः । महानजादिर्मन इन्द्रियाणि । सर्वेन्द्रियार्था विबुधाश्च सर्वे । ये हेतवस्ते जगतोऽङ्गभूताः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūstoyamagniḥ pavanaṃ khamādiḥmahānajādirmana indriyāṇisarvendriyārthā vibudhāśca sarveye hetavaste jagato'ṅgabhūtāḥ
translation: [1] All the causes of the evolution of the world such as (the elements) the earth, water, fire, air, the sky and its originator-ahaṃkāra, the principle of cosmic intelligence called mahat, prakṛti (Primordial matter) and the cause thereof (viz. Puruṣa, the mind, the senses (and organs) and their objects and all the gods presiding over them, are all evolved out of your Supreme Person (Śrī mūrti).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā presents the viśiṣṭādvaita stance: He shows that this verse described the Lord both as the material cause as well as the efficient cause. “From Your Person Brahman characterised by mūla-prakṛti as his body is transformed into mahat, ahaṃkāra.”

devanagari: नैते स्वरूपं विदुरात्मनस्ते । ह्यजादयोऽनात्मतया गृहीताः । अजोऽनुबद्धः स गुणैरजाया । गुणात् परं वेद न ते स्वरूपम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: naite svarūpaṃ vidurātmanastehyajādayo'nātmatayā gṛhītāḥajo'nubaddhaḥ sa guṇairajāyāguṇāt paraṃ veda na te svarūpam
translation: These—god Brahma[2] and others—being material (or other-than-the spirit) do not know the essential nature of yours who are the real ātman (Supreme self). For god Brahmā is conditioned by or enveloped in the guṇas of Prakṛti. As such he is unable to congnize your essential nature which is beyond all guṇas.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī: Jīva and others—Like inanimate things, even jīva cannot cognize your nature. The jīvas are observed with the false notion that the body and soul are identical or they themselves are the absolute masters.
Padaratnāvalī These—Brahmā and others cannot cognize Your real nature as they are distinct and different from your self.

devanagari: त्वां योगिनो यजन्त्यद्धा महापुरुषमीश्वरम् । साध्यात्मं साधिभूतं च साधिदैवं च साधवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tvāṃ yogino yajantyaddhā mahāpuruṣamīśvaramsādhyātmaṃ sādhibhūtaṃ ca sādhidaivaṃ ca sādhavaḥ
translation: Certainly, the righteous and practitioners of yoga worship you directly as the Supreme Person, the Ruler of the Universe—as the Indweller of the body (Or in the senses and organs of the bodies of every jīva), presiding over all material bodies (or the great elements like the ether, air, fire etc.), presiding over the bodies of god.

devanagari: त्रय्या च विद्यया केचित् त्वां वै वैतानिका द्विजाः । यजन्ते विततैर्यज्ञैः नानारूपामराख्यया ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: trayyā ca vidyayā kecit tvāṃ vai vaitānikā dvijāḥyajante vitatairyajñaiḥ nānārūpāmarākhyayā
translation: Some twice-borns (e g. Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas) follow the path of action—Karma mārga, and betake themselves to the course of sacrificial performance. With the help of the knowledge of three Vedas (Ṛk, Sāman and Yajus), they worship none but you under the names and forms of different divinities in extensive sacrificial sessions.

devanagari: एके त्वाखिलकर्माणि सन्न्यस्योपशमं गताः । ज्ञानिनो ज्ञानयज्ञेन यजन्ति ज्ञानविग्रहम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: eke tvākhilakarmāṇi sannyasyopaśamaṃ gatāḥjñānino jñānayajñena yajanti jñānavigraham
translation: Having renounced all activities[3] (pertaining to (karma- mārga, they have attained tranquillity and control of senses.[4] Such men of spiritual wisdom worship the Embodiment of Knowledge through sacrifice in the form of spiritual knowledge i.e. meditation on the Lord).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: give up all Vedic rites performed for attaining their desires which give rise to egotism and passions
OR: realize that Vedic rites are a form of worshipping the Lord.

Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī: Unswerving devotion of the Lord exclusively.

devanagari: अन्ये च संस्कृतात्मानो विधिनाभिहितेन ते । यजन्ति त्वन्मयास्त्वां वै बहुमूर्त्येकमूर्तिकम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: anye ca saṃskṛtātmāno vidhinābhihitena teyajanti tvanmayāstvāṃ vai bahumūrtyekamūrtikam
translation: [5] According to the injunctions laid by you (in the (Pañcarātra āgama) some consecrate their person[6] become absorbed in you and worship you as per procedure laid down by you (in the Pañcarātra āgama), as the One Deity abiding in many emanations (Vyūhas such as Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa. Pradyumna and Aniruddha) or as one form (viz. Nārāyaṇa).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
After describing the paths of Karma, Sāṅkhya, and Yoga, he mentions Pañcarātra and Śaiva systems in verses 7 and 8.

Footnote [6]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: as per Vaiṣṇavite and Śaivite procedures.
Bhāgavata Candrikā: By the five consecrating rites prescribed in the Pañcarātra āgamas.
Siddhāntapradīpa: quotes a verse enumerating the five saṃskāras; tāpaḥ puṇḍraṃ tathā nāma mantro yāgaś ca pañcamaḥ
tāpa is branding with representations of discus etc., Puṇḍra—mark on the forehead (the rest is easy).

devanagari: त्वामेवान्ये शिवोक्तेन मार्गेण शिवरूपिणम् । बह्वाचार्यविभेदेन भगवन् समुपासते ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvāmevānye śivoktena mārgeṇa śivarūpiṇambahvācāryavibhedena bhagavan samupāsate
translation: Others (i.e. devotees of Lord Śiva) adore you only in the form of Śiva[7] as per procedure ordained by god Śiva (in Śaiva āgamas) but with difference of details according to the teachers (of various Śaiva sects like Kāpālika Kālāmukha, Pāśupata etc.), Oh glorious Lord!
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Subodhinī In Tāmasa Kalpa Viṣṇu assumes the form of Śiva.

devanagari: सर्व एव यजन्ति त्वां सर्वदेवमयेश्वरम् । येऽप्यन्यदेवताभक्ता यद्यप्यन्यधियः प्रभो ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sarva eva yajanti tvāṃ sarvadevamayeśvaramye'pyanyadevatābhaktā yadyapyanyadhiyaḥ prabho
translation: [8] Those who are votaries of other deities and have thus reposed their faith and devotion unto them (thinking these deities as different from you), all of them really worship you who pervade them all, Oh Supreme Lord.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Gf. yepyanya-devatābhaktā yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ /
tepi māmeva kaunteya yajantyavidhipūrvakam //
  —Bhagavad Gītā 9.23.

devanagari: यथाद्रिप्रभवा नद्यः पर्जन्यापूरिताः प्रभो । विशन्ति सर्वतः सिन्धुं तद्वत्त्वां गतयोऽन्ततः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yathādriprabhavā nadyaḥ parjanyāpūritāḥ prabhoviśanti sarvataḥ sindhuṃ tadvattvāṃ gatayo'ntataḥ
translation: Oh Lord I Just as rivers with their sources in mountains and flooded by raining clouds enter into the sea from all directions, similarly all paths (of devotion and worship) lead ultimately unto you.

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति भवतः प्रकृतेर्गुणाः । तेषु हि प्राकृताः प्रोता आब्रह्मस्थावरादयः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti bhavataḥ prakṛterguṇāḥteṣu hi prākṛtāḥ protā ābrahmasthāvarādayaḥ
translation: Sattva, rajas and tamas are nothing but modifications of your Prakṛti (potency). And everything from inanimate immovables to the highest deity Brahmā, are the products of Prakṛti and are woven warf and woof in Prakṛti[9] (and therefore not different from you. You pervade them all)
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Or: and ultimately they merge into Prakṛti and Prakṛti unto you. In this way all gods etc. are absorbed in you.

devanagari: तुभ्यं नमस्तेऽस्त्वविषक्तदृष्टये । सर्वात्मने सर्वधियां च साक्षिणे । गुणप्रवाहोऽयमविद्यया कृतः । प्रवर्तते देवनृतिर्यगात्मसु ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tubhyaṃ namaste'stvaviṣaktadṛṣṭayesarvātmane sarvadhiyāṃ ca sākṣiṇeguṇapravāho'yamavidyayā kṛtaḥpravartate devanṛtiryagātmasu
translation: Hail to you whose mind is perfectly unattached to everything even though you are the In-dwelling soul of everybody and witness to the intellects (minds) of all. This stream of guṇas (saṃsāra) created by your Nescience operates powerfully on gods, men, and subhuman beings.

devanagari: अग्निर्मुखं तेऽवनिरङ्घ्रिरीक्षणं । सूर्यो नभो नाभिरथो दिशः श्रुतिः । द्यौः कं सुरेन्द्रास्तव बाहवोऽर्णवाः । कुक्षिर्मरुत् प्राणबलं प्र...
english_devnagari: agnirmukhaṃ te'vaniraṅghrirīkṣaṇaṃsūryo nabho nābhiratho diśaḥ śrutiḥdyauḥ kaṃ surendrāstava bāhavo'rṇavāḥkukṣirmarut prāṇabalaṃ prakalpitam
translation: Fire is regarded as your mouth, the earth as your feet, the sun as your eye, the sky as your navel, cardinal points as your ears, the celestial region (the firmament) as your head, Indra, the ruler of gods your arms, and oceans as your belly and the wind as your vital strength[10].
purport:
Footnote [10]:
VT. The verse shows how the Lord pervades all gods.

devanagari: रोमाणि वृक्षौषधयः शिरोरुहा । मेघाः परस्यास्थिनखानि तेऽद्रयः । निमेषणं रात्र्यहनी प्रजापतिः । मेढ्रस्तु वृष्टिस्तव वीर्यमिष्यते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: romāṇi vṛkṣauṣadhayaḥ śiroruhāmeghāḥ parasyāsthinakhāni te'drayaḥnimeṣaṇaṃ rātryahanī prajāpatiḥmeḍhrastu vṛṣṭistava vīryamiṣyate
translation: Trees and herbs are the hair on your body; the clouds are the locks of hair on your head; mountains are the bones and nails of your transcendental self; days and the nights are your winking; god Brahmā, the Greater of the world, is your generative organ, and rain shower is regarded as your semen.

devanagari: त्वय्यव्ययात्मन् पुरुषे प्रकल्पिता । लोकाः सपाला बहुजीवसङ्कुलाः । यथा जले सञ्जिहते जलौकसो । ऽप्युदुम्बरे वा मशका मनोमये ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvayyavyayātman puruṣe prakalpitālokāḥ sapālā bahujīvasaṅkulāḥyathā jale sañjihate jalaukaso'pyudumbare vā maśakā manomaye
translation: Oh Immortal Supreme Self! You are the perfect immutable Person who can be comprehended by the mind only. On you are projected worlds teeming with beings along with the protectors of those worlds, just as acquatic beings move in water or insect exists in the Udumbara fruit (Indian fig).

devanagari: यानि यानीह रूपाणि क्रीडनार्थं बिभर्षि हि । तैरामृष्टशुचो लोका मुदा गायन्ति ते यशः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāni yānīha rūpāṇi krīḍanārthaṃ bibharṣi hitairāmṛṣṭaśuco lokā mudā gāyanti te yaśaḥ
translation: Whatever forms you assume for your sportive activities in the world, dispel the afflictions and sorrows of people. Hence they sing your glories in joy.

devanagari: नमः कारणमत्स्याय प्रलयाब्धिचराय च । हयशीर्ष्णे नमस्तुभ्यं मधुकैटभमृत्यवे ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ kāraṇamatsyāya pralayābdhicarāya cahayaśīrṣṇe namastubhyaṃ madhukaiṭabhamṛtyave
translation: I bow to you the Fish incarnation, the cause of the world who moved freely in the cosmic waters of the ocean of deluge. Hail to you who assumed the form of Hayagrīva (horsenecked god) for killing the demons Madhu and Kaiṭabha.

devanagari: अकूपाराय बृहते नमो मन्दरधारिणे । क्षित्युद्धारविहाराय नमः सूकरमूर्तये ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: akūpārāya bṛhate namo mandaradhāriṇekṣityuddhāravihārāya namaḥ sūkaramūrtaye
translation: Salutations to you Oh gigantic Tortoise, the bearer of mountain Mandara (at the time of churning the ocean for nectar). I bow to you who have assumed the Boar form for the sportive activity of uplifting the earth (from the bottom of the ocean).

devanagari: नमस्तेऽद्‍भुतसिंहाय साधुलोकभयापह । वामनाय नमस्तुभ्यं क्रान्तत्रिभुवनाय च ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste'd‍bhutasiṃhāya sādhulokabhayāpahavāmanāya namastubhyaṃ krāntatribhuvanāya ca
translation: Oh Lord! The Dispeller of the fear of the righteous! I bow to you the wonderful man-lion. My salutations to you Oh Vāmana who have crossed the three worlds (in one step).

devanagari: नमो भृगूणां पतये दृप्तक्षत्रवनच्छिदे । नमस्ते रघुवर्याय रावणान्तकराय च ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: namo bhṛgūṇāṃ pataye dṛptakṣatravanacchidenamaste raghuvaryāya rāvaṇāntakarāya ca
translation: Salutations to you Paraśurāma, the chief of Bhṛgus who cut down the forest in the form of haughty Kṣatriyas. I salute to you, Oh Rāmacandra, the best of Raghus, who have killed Rāvaṇa.

devanagari: नमस्ते वासुदेवाय नमः सङ्कर्षणाय च । प्रद्युम्नायनिरुद्धाय सात्वतां पतये नमः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste vāsudevāya namaḥ saṅkarṣaṇāya capradyumnāyaniruddhāya sātvatāṃ pataye namaḥ
translation: Hail to you the Lord of Sātvatas! Who manifested yourself in four divine emanations of Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa (Balarāma), Pradyumna (Kṛṣṇa’s son) and Aniruddha (Pradyumna’s son).

devanagari: नमो बुद्धाय शुद्धाय दैत्यदानवमोहिने । म्लेच्छप्रायक्षत्रहन्त्रे नमस्ते कल्किरूपिणे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: namo buddhāya śuddhāya daityadānavamohinemlecchaprāyakṣatrahantre namaste kalkirūpiṇe
translation: Hail to you Lord Buddha! Who are absolutely pure but who deluded Daityas and Dānavas (by your anti-Vedic teaching). I bow to you who assumed the form of Kalki and killed the kṣatriyas who were reduced to the stage of Mlecchas (non-Aryans).

devanagari: भगवन् जीवलोकोऽयं मोहितस्तव मायया । अहं ममेत्यसद्‍ग्राहो भ्राम्यते कर्मवर्त्मसु ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavan jīvaloko'yaṃ mohitastava māyayāahaṃ mametyasad‍grāho bhrāmyate karmavartmasu
translation: Oh Glorious Lord! This world of beings is deluded by your māyā potency and wanders in the path of karma (saṃsāra) with the false notion of me-and-mine i.e. of the body being his own self and property as his own.

devanagari: अहं चात्मात्मजागार दारार्थस्वजनादिषु । भ्रमामि स्वप्नकल्पेषु मूढः सत्यधिया विभो ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ cātmātmajāgāra dārārthasvajanādiṣubhramāmi svapnakalpeṣu mūḍhaḥ satyadhiyā vibho
translation: And I am such a stupid person, Oh Lord Kṛṣṇa! That I too wander (move in) my body, sons, house, wives, wealth, kith and kins etc. who are really unreal like dream but thinking them to be real.

devanagari: अनित्यानात्मदुःखेषु विपर्ययमतिर्ह्यहम् । द्वन्द्वारामस्तमोविष्टो न जाने त्वात्मनः प्रियम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: anityānātmaduḥkheṣu viparyayamatirhyahamdvandvārāmastamoviṣṭo na jāne tvātmanaḥ priyam
translation: I entertain a contrary notion towards things which are not eternal (e.g. fruit of karmas), are not the real self (viz. my body) as well as which are full of troubles (e.g. house, property) and regard them as eternal, real-self and full of happiness). I am enveloped in the darkness of ignorance and take pleasure in the opposite pairs like pleasure and pain and I do not know that you are the most dear to me.

devanagari: यथाबुधो जलं हित्वा प्रतिच्छन्नं तदुद्‍भवैः । अभ्येति मृगतृष्णां वै तद्वत्त्वाहं पराङ्मुखः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yathābudho jalaṃ hitvā praticchannaṃ tadud‍bhavaiḥabhyeti mṛgatṛṣṇāṃ vai tadvattvāhaṃ parāṅmukhaḥ
translation: Just as an ignorant person does not know that it is really water that is covered by weeds and other water-plants, and abandoning the real water, runs after a mirage, similarly I have turned my face away from you (and am pursuing unrealities like my body, house etc.)

devanagari: नोत्सहेऽहं कृपणधीः कामकर्महतं मनः । रोद्धुं प्रमाथिभिश्चाक्षैः ह्रियमाणमितस्ततः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: notsahe'haṃ kṛpaṇadhīḥ kāmakarmahataṃ manaḥroddhuṃ pramāthibhiścākṣaiḥ hriyamāṇamitastataḥ
translation: Being of poor intellectual capacity and understanding, I am not able to control my mind which is perturbed by desire for enjoyment and activities to attain it, and am dragged here and there by uncontrollable senses.

devanagari: सोऽहं तवाङ्घ्र्युपगतोऽस्म्यसतां दुरापं । तच्चाप्यहं भवदनुग्रह ईश मन्ये । पुंसो भवेद् यर्हि संसरणापवर्गः । त्वय्यब्जनाभ सदुपासनया मतिः ...
english_devnagari: so'haṃ tavāṅghryupagato'smyasatāṃ durāpaṃtaccāpyahaṃ bhavadanugraha īśa manyepuṃso bhaved yarhi saṃsaraṇāpavargaḥtvayyabjanābha sadupāsanayā matiḥ syāt
translation: Oh Lord! I (who am as described above) have approached your feet which are not easily accessible to the unrighteous, regard (the accessibility of your feet) as an act of Grace on your part. Oh God with a lotus in the nevel! Release from the cycle of births and deaths is possible if one’s mind becomes attached to you through the service of saintly persons[11].
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: It is through Your Grace that one gets the opportunity of rendering service to saintly persons which leads to attachment to you, resulting in Final Emancipation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: नमो विज्ञानमात्राय सर्वप्रत्ययहेतवे । पुरुषेशप्रधानाय ब्रह्मणेऽनन्तशक्तये ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namo vijñānamātrāya sarvapratyayahetavepuruṣeśapradhānāya brahmaṇe'nantaśaktaye
translation: I bow to you who are the embodiment of Pure Consciousness and the Source of all knowledge. You are the Ruler (of factors e.g. Time-Spirit, Nature, Destiny) that control Jīvas; you are the very Brahman of infinite power.

devanagari: नमस्ते वासुदेवाय सर्वभूतक्षयाय च । हृषीकेश नमस्तुभ्यं प्रपन्नं पाहि मां प्रभो ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: namaste vāsudevāya sarvabhūtakṣayāya cahṛṣīkeśa namastubhyaṃ prapannaṃ pāhi māṃ prabho
translation:
Salutations to Lord Vāsudeva, the asylum of all beings. Oh Lord of sense-organs! I bow down to you. Oh Lord! Be pleased to protect me who have approached your feet.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे अक्रूरस्तुतिर्नाम चत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe akrūrastutirnāma catvāriṃśo'dhyāyaḥ
